strengths: Provides detailed examples of research ATL skills (OPVL, Cornell Notes) informing the learning goal and validated via expert consultation
weaknesses: Does not link ATL skill application to improvements in the bookletâs clarity or usability with concrete iterations or user feedback
Communication ATL section lacks quantitative evidence (e.g., survey results, agronomist feedback statistics) to demonstrate impact
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: While the student demonstrates strong research processes and source analysis, they must explicitly show how ATL skills enhanced the productâs quality and supply quantitative data to substantiate communication and engagement claims.

strengths: Establishes a compelling personal interest by linking family coffee-farming lineage and internship research to the learning goal
Clearly describes a feasible, real-world product (a bilingual ten-page illustrated booklet) and identifies a distribution partnership with Mullege
weaknesses: Success criteria are not measurable (no target yield increase or farmer engagement numbers)
Action plan omits key development phases, detailed milestones, deadlines, resources and status updates
Access FM table contains incomplete entries (missing size testing methods, unspecified environmental metrics)
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student grounds the project in a rich personal context and outlines a realistic, impactful product, but needs to define specific, measurable success criteria, flesh out a fully sequenced action plan with deadlines and resources, and complete all evaluation table rows with testable details.

strengths: Offers a compelling reflection on learning Amharic to improve farmer communication, illustrating genuine skill acquisition and outcome
weaknesses: Reflection on personal growth remains generalâlacks concrete examples such as number of professional emails sent or meetings organized
Product evaluation table is incomplete and unsupported: missing cost receipts, user survey feedback, environmental waste metrics, and material durability test results
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student provides a meaningful example of language-learning impact, yet must ground reflections and product evaluations in specific evidence and complete all criteria to demonstrate thorough self-assessment and validate the bookletâs performance.

annotations.comment: You establish a compelling personal interest by linking your familyâs coffee farming lineage and internship research to your learning goal, effectively grounding your project in relevant context.
In B.ii you list ATL processes but donât link them to product quality improvements. Cite specific iterations or user feedback that show how ATL skills enhanced the bookletâs clarity or usability.
Your learning goal is clear, but you need measurable success criteria (e.g. target increase in yield or number of farmers reached) to track and evaluate achievement.
Your intended product is well described: a bilingual ten-page booklet with visuals and distribution via Mullege, showing feasibility and partnership for real-world impact.
Your action plan outlines initial steps but omits key phases for booklet development, design reviews and translation. Add milestones with deadlines, resources, and status updates for each stage.
The Access FM table has incomplete entries (e.g. Size testing methods are missing and some âEnvironmentâ details lack metrics). Complete each row with specific, testable criteria and methods.
You thoroughly describe how research ATL skills (OPVL, Cornell Notes) informed your learning goal, with concrete examples of source analysis and validation through expert consultation.
Your communication ATL section notes partial effectiveness but lacks quantitative evidence (e.g. farmer survey results or agronomist feedback statistics). Include data to demonstrate impact.

Your reflection on personal growth mentions ATL challenges but remains general. Add concrete examples (e.g. number of professional emails sent, meetings organized, deadlines met) to illustrate skill development.
Your demonstration of learning Amharic to support farmer communication is a compelling reflection on the impact of communication skills, highlighting both effort and outcome.

Your product evaluation table identifies strengths and gaps but some cells remain general or N/A. For each specification, provide evidence (e.g. cost receipts, user survey feedback) to substantiate evaluation against criteria.
âEnvironmentâ criteria lack data on materials waste or sustainability impact. Include measurements (e.g. pages recycled, percent biodegradable materials) to fully evaluate environmental success.
âMaterialâ evaluation should report results from field durability tests (e.g. booklet condition after one month in farm conditions) to confirm lamination and binding meet success criteria.
